channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Experts warn that Trump’s use of AI images pushes new boundaries
item.description: The Trump administration has not shied away from sharing AI-generated imagery online, embracing cartoonlike visuals and memes and promoting them on official White House channels.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: This company pioneered virtual therapy for OCD. Now it wants to do the same for PTSD
item.description: When Stephen Smith started NOCD 11 years ago, he wanted to build an app for people like himself—one of the 8.2 million Americans with obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD)—to track their symptoms and time their therapy exercises.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Barker, Fast Company

item.title: France just approved a social media ban for kids under 15
item.description: French lawmakers approved a bill banning social media for children under 15, paving the way for the measure to enter into force at the start of the next school year in September, as the idea of setting a minimum age for use of the platforms gains momentum across Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Video shows flames flying from NASA plane that touched down without landing gear
item.description: A NASA research plane has landed without its landing gear in Texas, sending plumes of flame behind it.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: This new privacy-focused phone service is designed to keep your phone from getting hacked
item.description: A privacy-centric cellphone carrier called Cape is now officially available across the United States, offering a unique set of features to protect users from surveillance and identity theft.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: To fix federal HR, Washington must stop rewarding failure
item.description: This is the "maintenance trap" in action: Agencies spend so much time and money keeping broken systems running that they can't afford to replace them. The post To fix federal HR, Washington must stop rewarding failure first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok, YouTube, and Meta are headed to court for a landmark trial over social media addiction
item.description: Three of the world’s biggest tech companies face a landmark trial in Los Angeles starting this week over claims that their platforms—Meta’s Instagram, ByteDance’s TikTok, and Google’s YouTube—deliberately addict and harm children.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Quantum computing for all! Microsoft releases open source tools to help get everyone up to speed on the next-gen technology
item.description: Microsoft releases open source Quantum Development Kit, integrating simulators, VS Code, GitHub Copilot, and workflows for chemistry and error correction.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Watch out! Lego's 5 new Smart Play sets might be more affordable, but they lack a crucial brick
item.description: I’ve been convinced Lego Smart Play is actually incredible, and all it took was some classic space jazz.
item.dc:creator: Hamish Hector, TechRadar

item.title: French authorities to ban Teams, Zoom, other video apps for gov’t use
item.description: France plans to phase out American video conferencing services such as Zoom and Microsoft Teams within government agencies and replace them with its own proprietary tool, Visio.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Anthropic cofounder Daniela Amodei says trusted enterprise AI will transcend the hype cycle
item.description: While Silicon Valley argues over bubbles, benchmarks, and who has the smartest model, Anthropic has been focused on solving problems that rarely generate hype but ultimately determine adoption: whether AI can be trusted to operate inside the world’s most sensitive systems.
item.dc:creator: Victor Dey, Fast Company

item.title: In Davos, warnings about AI and jobs
item.description: The impact of AI on the labor market is more serious than previously expected, with entry-level jobs collapsing and white-collar jobs in danger, participants at the World Economic Forum (WEF) said last week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Exclusive: This free VPN extension was hijacking CyberGhost's free servers – here's what it means for your privacy
item.description: TechRadar experts discovered that the 'BiuBiu VPN' Chrome extension was misusing CyberGhost’s resources. Here's all we know.
item.dc:creator: Chiara Castro, TechRadar

item.title: Meta, TikTok and YouTube face landmark trial over youth addiction claims
item.description: Jury selection starts this week in the LA County Superior Court. It’s the first time the companies will argue their case before a jury, and the outcome could have profound effects on their businesses.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Independent auditors confirm Surfshark’s VPN infrastructure as secure
item.description: Surfshark has completed a comprehensive independent security audit of its network infrastructure. SecuRing found no critical vulnerabilities, reinforcing the provider's claims of resilience against sophisticated cyberattacks.
item.dc:creator: Rene Millman, TechRadar

item.title: Reports of SATA’s demise are overblown, but the technology is aging fast
item.description: With the upheaval taking place in the memory market, a new rumor has emerged about the demise of the Serial Advanced Technology Attachment (SATA) drive interface.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ransomware attack exposes Social Security numbers at major gas station chain
item.description: Ransomware attack on Texas gas station chain exposes 377,000 people's Social Security numbers and driver's licenses. Keep your data safe with these steps.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intel nabs Qualcomm veteran to lead GPU initiative
item.description: Intel and Qualcomm have traded executives several times over the years, and the trading continues with Intel luring away Qualcomm’s GPU lead. Eric Demers, a long-time industry veteran GPU engineer, joins Intel as senior vice president and the lead of its GPU engineering team.
item.dc:creator: Network World
